en.value:
Provide Basic and Advance Training to farmers in cultivation of selected species of MAPs.
	Establish nurseries for MAPs and other fodder and tree species in project area.
	New MAPs/Multi-purpose Cooperative is formed
	Business development and networking linkage training for cooperative groups is provided
	The capacity of cooperatives is built through exposure visit
	Provide support to farmers to obtain “Product Origin Certificates” in cooperation with the District Forest Office.
	Provide support on value addition of MAPs Product.
	Provide training on Legal provision, Sustainable harvesting techniques, market management and network.
	Project inception, endorsement and review workshop in district level
	Project inception, endorsement and review workshop in VDCs level
	Project monitoring CPAC and DPAC
	Provide support for short-term skill development training for women and youth from socially and economically marginalized households.
	Provide support for Women’s Group for livestock based income-generating activities such as poultry, goat or pig farming.
	Provide training in community-based eco-tourism activities (such as home stay and lodge management, etc.)
	Provide support to Women's Groups for procuring seedlings of large cardamom
	Support the construction of multi-purpose cooperatives buildings.
	Support the construction of check dams to stabilize the landslide prone area.
	Support the construction of small scale Drinking Water System
	Build capacity of ‘community user groups’ in the entire cycle of planning, construction and maintenance of rural infrastructure.
	Identify vulnerable areas in the community and prioritize areas needing interventions
	Support reforestation of private and barren land with native and fast growing species.
	Train MAPs farmers, cooperative members and local NGOs in plant diversity monitoring techniques.
	Establish monitoring protocols for MAPs and appropriate number of monitoring blocks within Community Forests and degraded land.
	Raise awareness of importance of biodiversity protection through posters and pamphlets.
	Conduct NGO Capacity Assessment of local NGO partners.
	Support the capacity, knowledge and skills of NGO partner staff in management, program implementation, and monitoring and evaluation tools and techniques.
	Support the access to information and enhance knowledge of NGO partner staff in government and non-governmental support for earthquake-affected communities.
	Collect information at HH level about the status of legal documents (citizenship papers, land revenue document, bank documents, etc.) lost or destroyed during the earthquake in coordination with local level institutions (DDRC, VDC Secretary, Ward Citizen Forum, etc.).
	Organize orientations to NGO partners and local community groups on access to information about technical and financial support available for re-building.
	Provide support to vulnerable groups (female headed households, elderly population, people living with disabilities, etc.) in accessing support for re-building.
	Support the documentation of destruction of culturally valuable sites and infrastructure in earthquake affected areas in consultation with local communities.
	Develop a plan for the restoration of significant heritage sites that are important for the community that includes a cost plan.
	Based on the restoration plan seek funding for supporting the cultural restoration.

en.full: Building Resilient Livelihoods and Ecosystems in Remote Mountain Communities in Nepal (Pragatishil Pahad Project)

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
